name: Coals of fire
notes: Paul cites the wisdom of Proverbs on overcoming evil with good, telling the Roman believers to feed a hungry enemy and give the thirsty drink. Proverbs splits the counsel across two verses: the deed of mercy in 25:21 and its result, heaping coals of fire upon the enemy's head, in 25:22; Paul gathers both the deed and the result into the single sentence of Romans 12:20. The shared core is exact: caring for an enemy's hunger and thirst, and the promised heaping of coals of fire upon his head. Proverbs adds that the LORD shall reward the doer, a vantage Paul leaves to the surrounding exhortation rather than quoting; this added clause is left as a matter for further study.

links.label: thou shalt heap coals of fire ... his head
